title: Trading Bot
author: edgarmoria
selftext: Guys, I've developed a trading bot that does on average 17k a month with a max drawdown of 1.2k. It's perfect for prop firms. I'm thinking of creating a website to sell it on a subscription basis. The bot has zero lookahead and it's solely based on mathematical algorithms and price action. How much would you be willing to pay for it? Would you like a 7 day trial? While I don't need the subscription money I also don't want to release it to the general public and I've spent around 12k and lots of sweat and tears blowing up accounts until I fine tuned. Renting it out is just hypothetical at this time but it would be nice to know if the public would be willing to pay for it. I know there's a lot of bullshit out there selling snake oil so not sure if creating a website and selling it would be worth my time. It would be nice to give something back tho.

comments.body: I think you need more social proof and the 7 day trial is great for this. Can your bot realistically pass an account in 5 trading days or less?

replies.author: edgarmoria
replies.body: It did 5k since last Tuesday, but it really depends. It cuts of trading for the day at -350. It lost 2 days in a row in the past, so it basically lost 700 in two days. Sometimes it does 3K in a day. It did more than 3k every week independently of losses which is good enough to pass a 50k eval. But with the 50% consistency it may take more than a week (less than two for sure).

comments.author: ThebobostorePakistan
comments.body: i am testing bots from the past 3 years, actually you can call me an algo trader :) till now whatever i tested is mostly like win for a week then loose everything in a day, so there is no capital protection in most of the bots, in the past 3 years, i find only 1 or 2 bots which actually work in long run and protect the capital with proper stoploss. if you have proper stoploss with it then it will work. for me the best i used till now is smartedge. they offer i think 1 month free trial, so you should also give long trials to make people beleive.

replies.author: edgarmoria
replies.body: The bot/strategy offers a load of tools for customisation.  Still a work in progress but it's been profitable for the past year according to backtesting results and for the past 3 months live trading. The stop loss can be as tight or as loose as you choose to. Too tight is not recommended judging by backtesting results. Sometimes it's better to have 2 or 3 big losses than 35 small ones. Also if it's too tight you'll lose many good setups.

replies.author: ThebobostorePakistan
replies.body: The problem is that 1 year backtesting means nothing, becuase there is no big hits in the past 1 year, for instance the covid period was a hit, also for different pairs, different hits occure. so 1 year backtest is very small untill its positve on the backtest for 5-10 years. as i said, i am testing bots from the past 3 years, and till now i find only 1 or 2 bots which really work with capital protection even in harsh times.

replies.author: edgarmoria
replies.body: The bot is made specially thinking of prop firms. It opens and closes positions daily and closes off at 4.10pm. In fact this is a long only bot and it does very well in bearish markets. A lot better in bullish ones of course, but still. Sharpe Ratio of 1.8. It's pretty good. Not trying to sell it to anyone. At this stage I'm just checking if there's a viable market out there or if the snake oil seller completely ruin it already.

replies.author: ThebobostorePakistan
replies.body: if you are selling something valuable, you shouldnt be worried of the scammers, becuase they cant stay in the market for long. but for instance, i know EA's who they give long trials just to satisfy the customer before it make decesion to buy it, also securing the main EA from EA stealers is another problem in the market. as i said i am testing algos from the past 3 years, i feel you are at the initial stages and it will take some more time.

replies.author: edgarmoria
replies.body: I've been trading for 10 years. I think I would know what an initial stage would be.

comments.author: edgarmoria
comments.body: Hi everyone! I'm currently in the process of making the website. I'm really happy with how the bot is performing lately. This week it did 5k trading with 6 micros with a max drawdown of 500usd on live trading. It's setup on 1m on MES1! And MNQ1!. On MNQ1! it took 16 trades and had a 100% profit rate and on MES1! It took 20 trades and had an 85% profit rate. I feel I can bump the number of contracts up and easily double the profit while still keeping an acceptable drawdown and risk management, but slow and steady wins the race, so I'll keep it in test mode for a few more months. If you want me to let you know when the website is live please visit automatedalgos.com.
